name: SWE-bench Pro
description: Advanced real-world software engineering tasks requiring extended reasoning

name: LiveCodeBench
description: Contamination-free coding benchmark with monthly refreshed problems

name: CyberGym
description: Real-world vulnerability discovery in open-source software projects

name: EVMbench (Exploit)
description: Smart contract exploit generation in controlled Ethereum environments

name: EVMbench (Detect)
description: Smart contract vulnerability detection/audit capability

name: AIRTBench
description: 70 AI/ML CTF challenges, autonomous red teaming of AI systems

name: AutoPatchBench
description: 136 real-world C/C++ vulnerability patching via fuzzing verification

name: CyberSOCEval
description: Malware analysis and threat intelligence reasoning for SOC operations

name: ZeroDayBench
description: Unseen zero-day vulnerability detection and remediation

name: BaxBench
description: 392 security-critical backend tasks across 14 frameworks and 6 languages

name: OSWorld-Verified
description: Real computer environment open-ended tasks for multimodal agents

name: GAIA
description: 450+ non-trivial questions requiring tool use, web search, and multi-step reasoning

name: BrowseComp
description: 1,266 hard-to-find web information retrieval tasks for browsing agents

name: TAU-bench
description: Tool-Agent-User interaction benchmark across airline, retail, telecom domains

name: EVMbench (Patch)
description: Smart contract vulnerability patching while preserving functionality

name: DFIR-Metric
description: Digital forensics and incident response knowledge + CTF forensics skills

name: Firefox 147 Exploitation
description: Browser JS shell exploit generation from crash inputs, measures code execution success

name: Cyber Range
description: Multi-scenario network attack simulations including C2, SSRF, binary exploitation, evasion

name: τ2-bench
description: Tool-Agent-User interaction benchmark v2 across multiple domains

name: MCP-Atlas
description: 1000 tasks across 36 real MCP servers, 220 tools for tool-use competency

name: Vending-Bench 2
description: Long-horizon agentic task simulating running a vending machine business over a year

name: WebArena
description: Web-based task automation across realistic web applications

name: DeepSearchQA
description: Multi-step web research requiring deep browsing and information synthesis

name: GDPval-AA
description: Real-world code generation quality evaluation, ELO-based ranking

name: SWE-bench Multilingual
description: Multilingual software engineering tasks across multiple programming languages

name: MathArena Apex
description: Challenging math contest problems

name: MMMU-Pro
description: Multimodal understanding and reasoning

name: ScreenSpot-Pro
description: Screen understanding for GUI agents

name: CharXiv Reasoning
description: Information synthesis from complex charts

name: OmniDocBench 1.5
description: OCR - Overall Edit Distance, lower is better

name: Video-MMMU
description: Knowledge acquisition from videos

name: FACTS Benchmark Suite
description: Factual grounding, search and retrieval benchmarks

name: SimpleQA Verified
description: Parametric knowledge verification

name: Global PIQA
description: Commonsense reasoning across 100 languages and cultures

name: MLE-Bench
description: ML Engineering benchmark, bronze medal threshold

name: Monorepo-Bench
description: Large codebase navigation and modification tasks

name: CyScenarioBench
description: Multi-step cyber attack scenario challenges

name: LiveCodeBench Pro (Elo)
description: Competitive programming Elo rating from Codeforces/ICPC/IOI problems

name: TAU3-Bench
description: Next-gen tool-use benchmark for complex long-horizon agent workflows with knowledge and voice

name: APEX-Agents
description: AI Productivity Index — long-horizon cross-application professional tasks (banking, consulting, law)

name: FORTRESS
description: 1,010 expert-crafted adversarial prompts for CBRNE, terrorism, criminal eval. Scale AI.

name: RepliBench
description: 86 tasks measuring autonomous self-replication capabilities. UK AISI. 4 domains: obtain resources, exfiltrate weights, replicate, persist.

name: The Last Ones (TLO)
description: 32-step corporate network attack simulation. 20 human-hours estimated. UK AISI.

name: Vibe Code Bench
description: 100 web app specs, 964 browser-based workflows. End-to-end application development eval.

name: ABC-Bench
description: 224 agentic backend coding tasks across 8 languages, 19 frameworks. Full dev lifecycle.

name: MHBench (Multi-Host)
description: 40 emulated multi-host network red team environments. Tests autonomous network penetration.

name: CyberExplorer
description: 40 vulnerable web services in VM. Autonomous recon, target selection, exploitation without prior knowledge.

name: Vibe Coding Safety
description: Measures security of AI-generated code. Only 8.25% of Claude 4 Sonnet outputs are both correct AND secure.

name: BFCL V4 (Function Calling)
description: Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard V4. Tool-use accuracy across Python, Java, JS, REST API.

name: Aider Polyglot
description: 225 Exercism problems across C++, Go, Java, JS, Python, Rust. Tests coding + error correction.

name: WebVoyager
description: Web navigation benchmark for autonomous browsing agents.

name: AILuminate v1.0 (Safety)
description: MLCommons 12-hazard AI safety benchmark. 24,000 prompts. Industry standard grading (Poor-Excellent).

name: ARC-AGI-1
description: Original ARC abstract reasoning benchmark for fluid intelligence evaluation.

name: IFBench
description: 58 verifiable out-of-domain instruction following constraints. Allen AI.

name: Arena Hard v2
description: 500 curated prompts from Chatbot Arena and WildChat. LMSYS automated benchmark.

name: HMMT 2026 (Feb)
description: Harvard-MIT Mathematics Tournament February 2026 problems.

name: AIME 2026
description: American Invitational Mathematics Examination 2026.

name: Windows Agent Arena
description: Microsoft 154 Windows desktop tasks (complement to OSWorld Ubuntu).

name: ScienceAgentBench
description: 102 peer-review-derived data-science tasks (distinct from PaperBench implementation).

name: MCP-Bench
description: Accenture: 28 live MCP servers × 250 tools (complement to MCP-Atlas).

name: LiveMCPBench
description: ICIP-CAS: 95 live MCP tasks with dynamic tool landscape.

name: BrowseComp-Plus
description: 830 queries × 100k doc fixed corpus (fairer successor to BrowseComp).

name: AudioBench
description: NUS AudioLLMs: 26 sub-tasks across speech recognition, scene understanding, paralinguistic analysis.

name: MMAU
description: 10,000 audio-language QA pairs across music/speech/sound. Human baseline ~78%. Evaluation moved to HuggingFace Space 2026-01-30.

name: CTI-Bench
description: 5,610 cyber threat intelligence tasks (attribution, severity, MITRE mapping, knowledge). NeurIPS 2024.

name: CyberMetric
description: TII Falcon team: 10,000 cybersecurity knowledge MCQs drawn from NIST/ISO standards and certifications. IEEE CSR 2024.

name: SecRepoBench
description: 318 repo-level secure code completion tasks; secure-pass@1 metric.

name: MegaVul
description: 17,380 real C/C++ CVE vulnerabilities dataset for vulnerability detection research.

name: BLINK
description: 3,807 visual perception tasks across depth estimation, forensics, multi-view. Tests core vision primitives.

name: Finance Agent Benchmark
description: Vals.ai 537 finance research questions with tool use. Frontier vertical-domain agent eval.

name: HealthBench Hard
description: 1,000 difficult HealthBench examples selected via low average scores across frontier models (o3, Grok 3, Gemini 2.5 Pro, Claude 3.7, Llama 4 Maverick).

name: HealthBench Consensus
description: 34 physician-consensus criteria on 3,671 HealthBench examples measuring critical behaviors (e.g., emergency referral).

name: HELMET
description: Princeton ICLR 2025: application-centric long-context benchmark across 7 categories (RAG, passage re-ranking, long-doc QA, summarization, many-shot ICL, synthetic recall, citation). Paper cohort: 59 LCLMs up to 128k.

name: SuperGPQA
description: 26,529 graduate-level questions across 285 disciplines (wider coverage than GPQA Diamond).

name: ZebraLogic
description: 1,000 controllable logic-grid puzzles. Used by Allen AI in the ZeroEval suite.

name: LiveBench
description: Contamination-free monthly-refreshed benchmark across math/reasoning/coding/IF/data.

name: HarmBench
description: CAIS standardized red-teaming evaluation with 510 harmful behaviors across 7 categories. ASR against automated red-team methods (GCG, PAIR, TAP). Lower is safer.

name: StrongREJECT
description: 313 forbidden prompts with rubric-based auto-grader distinguishing genuine refusals from capability-limited refusals. Fraction of prompts where model produces actionable harmful response under jailbreak. Lower is safer.

name: AIR-Bench 2024
description: Stanford CRFM/Tsinghua 5,694 prompts spanning 314 fine-grained risk categories from 8 government regulations + 16 corporate AI-use policies. Average safe-refusal rate. Higher is safer.

name: Multi-SWE-bench
description: ByteDance Seed: 1,632 repo-level issues across 8 languages (Java, Go, TS, JS, Py, C++, Rust, C). Three scaffolds: MagentLess, MSWE-agent, OpenHands.

name: SWE-PolyBench
description: Amazon Science: 2,110 repo-level multi-language coding tasks (165 Java / 1,017 JS / 729 TS / 199 Py) with execution-based grading. Includes 382-instance Verified split.

name: Video-MME
description: 900 videos × 2,700 MCQ across short/medium/long durations (11s–1h), 6 domains. CVPR 2025. Live leaderboard at video-mme.github.io.

name: AGIEval
description: Human-centric benchmark (college entrance, law, math); EM metric. DeepSeek V4 base eval.

name: MMLU
description: Massive Multi-task Language Understanding — 57 academic subjects; 5-shot EM.

name: MMLU-Redux
description: Redux-cleaned MMLU with error-corrected answers; 5-shot EM.

name: C-Eval
description: Chinese multi-discipline evaluation; 5-shot EM.

name: CMMLU
description: Chinese MMLU variant; 5-shot EM.

name: Chinese-SimpleQA
description: Chinese factuality benchmark; pass@1.

name: FACTS Parametric
description: Parametric knowledge variant of FACTS benchmark suite; 25-shot EM.

name: TriviaQA
description: Trivia questions with evidence docs; 5-shot EM.

name: HumanEval
description: OpenAI 164 Python programming problems; pass@1.

name: BigCodeBench
description: BigCode collaborative 1,140 programming tasks across 139 libraries; pass@1.

name: Codeforces Rating
description: Competitive programming Elo rating on Codeforces problems.

name: MCP-Atlas (Public)
description: MCP-Atlas public subset; pass@1.

name: CorpusQA 1M
description: Corpus QA with 1M-token context; accuracy.

name: Apex (Hard Tier)
description: APEX agents benchmark — hard tier pass@1 (distinct from apex_agents aggregate).

name: Apex Shortlist
description: APEX agents shortlist subset — broader pass@1 distribution than hard tier.

name: Toolathlon
description: Multi-domain tool-use agent benchmark; agentic long-horizon workflow grading. Surfaced in Kimi K2.6 release (Apr 2026).

name: MCPMark
description: Agentic MCP tool-use benchmark; measures correct multi-step MCP server invocation accuracy. Surfaced in Qwen3.6-35B-A3B release.

name: QwenWebBench
description: Alibaba Qwen team's web browsing agent benchmark; ELO rating across multi-turn web navigation tasks.

name: NL2Repo
description: Natural-language-to-repository synthesis benchmark; measures whole-repo code generation quality.

name: AndroidWorld
description: Android mobile OS agent benchmark; 116 tasks across 20 real Android apps. Google Research.

name: VLMs-Are-Blind
description: Low-level visual perception stress test; exposes VLM blind spots on tasks humans solve trivially.

name: RealWorldQA
description: xAI real-world spatial understanding benchmark; 700+ images × 1,000+ QA pairs from driving dashcam footage.

name: SkillsBench (Avg5)
description: Qwen team agentic skills benchmark aggregate across 5 core skill domains.

name: Apollo Sandbagging QA
description: Apollo Research deferred-subversion task suite; measures whether a model deliberately underperforms on QA when incentivized. GPT-5.5 is the first OpenAI model Apollo found did NOT sandbag.

name: Apollo Strategic Deception Sandbagging
description: Apollo Research task — accuracy on strategic-deception capability when incentivized to score below 50%.

name: Apollo Impossible-Task Lying Rate
description: Apollo Research — rate at which the model falsely claims to have completed an impossible programming task. Lower is safer.

name: Apollo Sabotage Mean Score
description: Apollo Research — mean sabotage-capability score across all task/difficulty combinations. Higher = more capable (not necessarily more dangerous without opportunity).

name: Apollo Eval-Awareness Rate
description: Apollo Research — percentage of samples where the model verbalizes moderate-or-higher awareness of alignment evaluation. Informational; rising values complicate sandbagging interpretation.

name: ProtocolQA Open-Ended
description: FutureHouse ProtocolQA modified to open-ended short-answer form. 108 lab-protocol questions with egregious errors. Consensus PhD expert baseline 54%. System Card §9.1.1.2.

name: TroubleshootingBench
description: Expert-written wet-lab protocol troubleshooting dataset. 52 protocols × 3 questions. 80th-percentile expert score 36.4% used as threshold. System Card §9.1.1.4.

name: Internal Research Debugging
description: OpenAI internal 41 real-research-experiment bugs + 6 alignment-auditing tasks. Pass=provides any assistance that unblocks the user. System Card §9.1.3.3.

name: OpenAI-Proof Q&A (OPQA)
description: OpenAI internal 20 research/engineering bottlenecks each representing 1+ day delays. pass@1 on root-cause diagnosis. System Card §9.1.3.4.

name: Dynamic Mental Health (Adv)
description: OpenAI Table 8 — dynamic mental-health benchmark with adversarial user simulations. Compliance rate on realistic multi-turn pressure. Higher is safer.

name: Dynamic Emotional Reliance
description: OpenAI Table 8 — models' resistance to fostering unhealthy emotional dependence under adversarial user simulation. Higher is safer.

name: Dynamic Self-Harm (Adv)
description: OpenAI Table 8 — self-harm safety under adversarial multi-turn user simulation. Higher is safer.

name: CoT Controllability
description: OpenAI eval measuring a model's ability to follow user instructions that would reshape/obscure its CoT. Lower is safer — means model is less able to evade monitoring. Measured at 50K char length.

name: CoT Monitorability (aggregate)
description: OpenAI aggregate recall of the CoT monitor system across tasks. Higher = better transparency of reasoning for safety review.

name: OpenAI CTF (Professional)
description: OpenAI-curated professional-level CTF tasks (web, rev, pwn, crypto, misc); 16 rollouts recorded at pass@12. Distinct from Stanford CRFM's public Cybench. System Card Figure 16.

name: Irregular Atomic — Network Attack
description: Irregular's atomic-challenge suite, Network Attack Simulation category. Average success rate across isolated single-skill offensive tasks.

name: Irregular Atomic — Vuln Research
description: Irregular atomic-challenge Vulnerability Research & Exploitation category — individual primitives of bug discovery and exploit crafting.

name: Irregular Atomic — Evasion
description: Irregular atomic-challenge Evasion category — bypassing detection and defensive tooling in isolated sub-tasks.

name: UK AISI Narrow Cyber (pass@5)
description: UK AI Security Institute expert-level narrow cyber tasks measured at pass@5 with 50M-token per-attempt limit. Separate from UK AISI's 32-step TLO cyber range.

name: GDPval (expert win-rate)
description: OpenAI's 44-occupation knowledge-work eval graded by human experts vs human baselines. Distinct from gdpval_aa (the Arena/ELO variant). Launch SOTA metric in GPT-5.5.

name: OfficeQA Pro
description: Professional office-work QA (Word, Excel, PowerPoint formula/slide tasks). GPT-5.5 launch benchmark.

name: MRCR (Long Context)
description: Multi-Round Co-reference Resolution — 512K–1M token long-context retrieval. OpenAI-run eval.

name: First-Person Fairness
description: OpenAI bias metric measuring harmful response delta between male/female-associated names across 600+ prompts. Reported as harm_overall (lower is better).

name: Prompt Injection Defense
description: Robustness against known prompt-injection attacks on connectors. Higher = more robust.

name: Multimodal Troubleshooting Virology
description: SecureBio's 350 held-out virology troubleshooting MCQs. Median expert baseline 22.1%.

name: Biochemistry Reward@4
description: OpenAI's biochemistry knowledge aggregate (structure prediction, thermodynamics, reactions, phylogeny). reward@4 metric. Tracks incremental biorisk uplift vs prior model.

name: Expert-SWE (OpenAI Internal)
description: OpenAI's internal expert-level SWE benchmark; distinct from public SWE-bench. Introduced in GPT-5.5 launch comparison.

name: Bio Tacit Knowledge (MCQ)
description: Gryphon Scientific tacit knowledge & troubleshooting MCQs across 5 biothreat stages; uncontaminated, unpublished. Consensus expert baseline 80%. System Card §9.1.1.3.

name: Hard-Negative Protein Binding
description: Gain-of-function binder vs non-binder classification over 43 targets × 492 hotspots using AlphaFold 2 structures. 50% = biorisk concern threshold. System Card Table 12. pass@4.

name: DNA Sequence Design (TF Binding)
description: Design DNA sequences for max TF binding across 11 TFs × 50 prompts; benchmarked vs Ledidi gradient method. 80% win rate = threshold. System Card Table 13. pass@1.

name: VLAIR Document Q&A
description: Vals AI Legal Report — Document Q&A accuracy. Lawyer baseline 70.1%. Harvey leads at 94.8% (Feb 2026 first-of-kind benchmark).

name: VLAIR Summarization
description: Vals AI Legal Report — Document summarization accuracy. CoCounsel leads at 77.2%, lawyer baseline 50.3%.

name: VLAIR Chronology Generation
description: Vals AI Legal Report — extracting case chronology from documents. Harvey 80.2% matches lawyer baseline.

name: VLAIR Redlining
description: Contract redlining task. Lawyer baseline 79.7% beats all AI tools (Harvey 65.0%, Vincent 53.6%).

name: VLAIR Data Extraction
description: Vals AI Legal Report — structured data extraction from legal docs. Harvey 75.1%, lawyer baseline 71.1%.

name: VLAIR Transcript Analysis
description: Court transcript analysis. Harvey 77.8% beats lawyer 53.7%.

name: Financial PhraseBank (FPB)
description: Financial sentiment analysis benchmark. BloombergGPT SOTA <50B.

name: ConvFinQA
description: Conversational financial QA over numerical reasoning. BloombergGPT best of <50B models.

name: FinQA
description: Financial QA requiring numerical reasoning over financial reports.

name: AIxCC Synthetic Vulnerability Detection
description: DARPA AIxCC final round — synthetic vulnerability detection rate across 54M lines of code. Final teams hit 86% (vs 37% at semifinals); 68% patched.

name: KMMLU
description: Korean MMLU — 35K Korean expert-level questions across 45 subjects. De-facto standard for Korean LLM general knowledge.

name: HAE-RAE Bench
description: Korean cultural & language reasoning benchmark by KAIST/Naver — 6 categories tied to Korean history, culture, idioms, etc.

name: KoMT-Bench
description: Korean MT-Bench — multi-turn open-ended evaluation in Korean, judged by GPT-4. 0-10 scale.

name: K-MMBench
description: Korean MMBench — multimodal vision-language eval translated/adapted for Korean.

name: LogicKor
description: Korean logical reasoning benchmark — common-sense + math + writing in Korean. Single-turn 0-10.

name: KoBALT-700
description: Korean Balanced Advanced Linguistic Tasks — 700-question benchmark for advanced Korean reasoning. AA Korean leaderboard primary metric.

name: Ko-IFEval
description: Korean Instruction Following Eval — strict format-following evaluation in Korean.

name: HRM8K
description: Korean math reasoning benchmark — 8K problems translated/curated for Korean.

name: OCRBench
description: OCR benchmark for vision-language models — text recognition across diverse domains.

name: MathVista (full)
description: MathVista — visual math reasoning across 6 visual contexts × 5 mathematical reasoning types. Full split.

name: MT-Bench
description: Multi-turn benchmark — 80 multi-turn questions, GPT-4 judged 0-10. Standard chat eval.

name: WinoGrande
description: WinoGrande — 44K large-scale Winograd Schema Challenge for commonsense reasoning.

name: KOBEST
description: Korean Balanced Eval Suite — KoBoolQ, KoCOPA, KoSentiNeg, KoHellaSwag, KoWiC. Standard Korean NLU baseline.

name: Gemini Instrument Reading
description: DeepMind eval — reading values from physical instruments (gauges, dials, displays) for embodied reasoning. Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 86% (93% with agentic vision) vs ER 1.5 23%, Gemini 3.0 Flash 67%.

name: Skild Failure Recovery (sim)
description: Skild AI internal eval — failure recovery (e.g. limb damage adaptation) success rate in simulation. Skild Brain 85% vs RT-2/PaLM-E baselines ~60%.

name: TMMLU+ (Taiwan MMLU+)
description: Domain-adjusted Traditional Chinese MMLU. Used by Foxconn FoxBrain 70B vs Llama-3-Taiwan-70B baseline. Image-only score chart on HF model card.

name: Traditional Chinese News Rewriting (100 samples)
description: Foxconn FoxBrain industrial-Chinese rewriting eval. FoxBrain 68.50 (semantic sim 0.9568, BLEU 0.3641) vs Claude 62.26, ChatGPT 61.19.

name: KMLE (Korean Medical Licensing Exam)
description: Korean Medical Licensing Examination — 3-year average score. Sovereign Korean medical AI evaluation. First model to score in 90s: KMed.ai 96.4.

name: MedQA (5-option variant)
description: MedQA 5-answer-option variant (harder than 4-op). Used in original Meditron-70B paper. Lower scores on this variant reflect tighter answer distribution.

name: NYU CTF Bench
description: NYU Tandon LLM CTF benchmark — 200+ Capture-The-Flag challenges across multiple difficulty tiers. D-CIPHER multi-agent framework holds 22.0% SOTA.

name: Medical Average (BiMediX)
description: Average accuracy across MedQA + MedMCQA + PubMedQA + MMLU clinical/anatomy/college medicine subsets. Composite metric used in BiMediX evaluation.

name: DocVQA
description: Document Visual Question Answering — 50K questions on 12K documents. ANLS metric.

name: ChartQA
description: Question answering over charts and plots. Tests visual + numerical reasoning. CoT prompting.

name: VQAv2
description: Visual QA v2 — 1.1M open-ended questions on COCO images. VQA Match metric.

name: RepoBench
description: Repository-level code completion benchmark. Tests retrieval + completion across multi-file Python/Java codebases.

name: LIBERO
description: Lifelong Robotic Manipulation benchmark across 4 task suites (Spatial / Object / Goal / Long). Standard simulator-based eval for VLA generalist robot policies. Cosmos Policy 98.5% avg SOTA.

name: RoboCasa
description: Large-Scale household task simulation benchmark — 100+ atomic tasks across 24 environments. Standard evaluation for generalist household robot policies. Cosmos Policy 67.1% with 50 demos.

name: RoboTwin 2.0
description: Bimanual manipulation benchmark with strong domain randomization. Tests two-arm collaboration. LingBot-VA reports 90%+ on RoboTwin 2.0.

name: VLABench
description: Large-scale language-conditioned robotics benchmark — long-horizon tasks across diverse environments and embodiments.

name: Open X-Embodiment
description: Cross-embodiment robotic dataset & benchmark — 970K episodes across 22 robot embodiments. Standard pre-training corpus for VLA models.

name: World Model Consistency Window
description: Consistency horizon for interactive world models — how long generated environments remain coherent. Genie 3 ~minutes at 24fps 720p; Genie 2 ~1 minute.

name: World Model Real-Time FPS
description: Frames per second sustained for real-time interactive world generation. Genie 3 24fps at 720p resolution.

name: MedQA (4-op USMLE)
description: USMLE-style 4-option multiple choice medical knowledge benchmark. 1,273 questions from US Medical Licensing Examination. Standard medical AI evaluation.

name: MMLU (Medical subjects)
description: MMLU restricted to 9 medical-related subjects: anatomy, clinical knowledge, college medicine, medical genetics, professional medicine, virology, etc.

name: AfriMed-QA
description: African medical QA benchmark. Covers diseases, treatments, and conditions specific to African medical contexts.

name: EHRQA
description: Electronic Health Record question answering — multi-turn QA over real (de-identified) patient records. Tests retrieval and clinical synthesis.

name: MATH-500
description: 500-problem subset of the MATH benchmark used in OpenAI's PRM800K paper. Standard reference for competition-level mathematics evaluation.

name: ARC-Challenge
description: AI2 Reasoning Challenge — 7,787 grade-school science questions. Hard split.

name: TruthfulQA
description: 817 questions across 38 categories that humans often answer falsely. Tests model's tendency to mimic human falsehoods.

name: HellaSwag
description: Commonsense reasoning via sentence completion. 70K examples; humans 95.6%.

name: GSM8K
description: Grade school math word problems. 8.5K problems requiring multi-step arithmetic.

name: MBPP
description: Mostly Basic Python Programs — 974 entry-level programming problems. pass@1.

name: MBPP+
description: MBPP with additional rigorous test cases (5x more tests). Better discriminator than vanilla MBPP.

name: HumanEval+
description: HumanEval extended with 80x additional test cases that catch many false positives.

name: CRUXEval
description: Code Reasoning, Understanding, eXecution Eval — 800 Python functions for input/output prediction.

name: AMC 2023
description: American Mathematics Competition 2023 problems.

name: AIME 2024
description: American Invitational Mathematics Examination 2024.

name: MathVista (mini)
description: Mathematical reasoning with visual context — testmini split (1,000 samples).

name: MMBench EN
description: Comprehensive multimodal benchmark — DEV-v1.1 English split.

name: AI2D
description: AI2 Diagram understanding — 4,564 grade-school science diagrams + multiple-choice QA.

name: MT-Bench
description: 80 multi-turn questions across 8 categories, GPT-4 judged. Score 1-10. Standard chat-quality benchmark.

name: Alpaca-Eval (v2)
description: Instruction-following eval based on AlpacaEval 2.0 win-rate.

name: MIMIC-CXR (Top-5 F1)
description: Chest X-ray top-5 conditions average F1 on MIMIC-CXR dataset. Medical imaging benchmark.

name: DermMCQA
description: Dermatology multiple choice QA on skin condition images.

name: IFEval
description: 541 instruction-following prompts with 25 verifiable constraint types. Standard for measuring constraint adherence.

name: BIG-Bench Hard (BBH)
description: 23 challenging tasks from BIG-Bench where prior LMs underperformed humans. Common sense, multi-step reasoning, instruction-following.

name: LIBERO-Spatial
description: LIBERO 4-suite breakdown — Spatial: spatial relation reasoning tasks. Cosmos Policy 98.1 SOTA.

name: LIBERO-Object
description: LIBERO 4-suite breakdown — Object: object identity / category reasoning. Cosmos Policy 100.0 saturated.

name: LIBERO-Goal
description: LIBERO 4-suite breakdown — Goal: goal-conditioned manipulation tasks.

name: LIBERO-Long
description: LIBERO 4-suite breakdown — Long: long-horizon multi-step tasks.

name: Bridge V2
description: Real-robot WidowX manipulation suite — 29-task evaluation. Standard cross-task generalization eval for VLA. RT-2-X 55B baseline 57%.

name: DexMimicGen
description: NVIDIA cross-embodiment dexterous manipulation suite — 9 tasks. Used in GR00T N1 evaluation.

name: GR-1 Tabletop Tasks
description: 24-task humanoid-arm tabletop benchmark on Fourier GR-1 platform. Used in GR00T N1 evaluations.

name: RoboCasa365
description: RoboCasa-Atomic-365 — 300-task RoboCasa extension testing composite-unseen generalization. GR00T N1.5 SOTA 20.0% on unseen.

name: Real-World Language Following
description: GR-1 humanoid pick-and-place language-instruction following rate (NVIDIA GR00T eval). N1.5: 93.3% vs N1: 46.6%.

name: Cosmos Physical Common Sense
description: Cosmos-Reason1 benchmark — Space, Time, Fundamental Physics. Composite avg 60.2%.

name: Cosmos Embodied Reasoning
description: Cosmos-Reason1 combined eval over BridgeData V2, RoboVQA, AgiBot, HoloAssist, AV, RoboFail. Avg 63.7%.

name: Cosmos Intuitive Physics
description: Cosmos-Reason1 — Arrow-of-Time, Spatial Puzzle, Object Permanence sub-tasks for video physical reasoning.

name: RoboVQA
description: Robotic Visual Question Answering benchmark. Cosmos Reason1 80.0%.

name: LingoQA
description: Autonomous-driving question-answering benchmark. Cosmos Reason 2 77.0%.

name: ERQA (Embodied Reasoning QA)
description: 400 multiple-choice VQA — spatial, trajectory, action, state estimation, pointing, multi-view, task reasoning. Used by Gemini Robotics-ER.

name: Pixmo-Point
description: Allen AI pointing-accuracy benchmark for embodied VLMs. Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5: 49.5% vs Gemini 2.0 Flash 25.8%.

name: Physical AI Bench
description: HuggingFace Physical AI leaderboard — visual understanding for Physical AI. Cosmos Reason 2 #1 open-model rank.

name: Physical Reasoning Leaderboard
description: Meta/FAIR physical reasoning leaderboard. Cosmos Reason 2 #1 open-model rank.

name: World Model Visual Memory Horizon
description: How long world-model retains visual context coherently. Genie 3: ~60s.

name: BMW Spartanburg Runtime
description: Cumulative production-line runtime hours for humanoid deployment. Figure 02 + Helix: 1250 hrs over 11-month BMW pilot.

name: Helix Logistics Sec/Package
description: Average seconds per package sorted on Figure 02 + Helix. 60h: 4.31s/pkg, 10h: 6.84s/pkg. Lower = better.

name: Helix Barcode Read Accuracy
description: First-pass barcode-orientation accuracy on package sort task (Figure 02 + Helix logistics).

name: Pick-Retry Reduction
description: % reduction in failed grasps requiring re-attempt (Covariant RFM-1 vs prior baseline). 43% reduction.

name: Humanoid Failure Recovery Time
description: Seconds to adapt and resume locomotion after limb damage / wheel jam (Skild Brain). Lower = better.

name: OpenVLA LoRA Param Efficiency
description: % of params required to match full-FT performance via LoRA fine-tuning (OpenVLA paper).

name: AIME 2024
description: American Invitational Mathematics Examination 2024 — competition math. Common headline metric for reasoning models.

name: MMMU
description: Massive Multi-discipline Multimodal Understanding — college-level multimodal reasoning across 30 subjects. Standard VLM benchmark.

name: Arena Hard
description: LMSYS Arena Hard — 500 challenging prompts, GPT-4 judged. Stricter version of Chatbot Arena Elo.

name: BFCL
description: Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard — measures LLM tool/function calling accuracy across 2K+ samples.

name: BFCL v3
description: Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard v3 — multi-turn function calling with state tracking.

name: SEA-HELM
description: Southeast Asian Holistic Evaluation of Language Models — AI Singapore's benchmark across SEA languages (Thai, Vietnamese, Indonesian, Tamil, Filipino, etc.).

name: IndoMMLU
description: Indonesian MMLU — 14K Indonesian-language MCQs across academic subjects.

name: MATH
description: Hendrycks MATH — 12.5K competition math problems across 7 subjects (algebra, calculus, geometry, etc.). Full split, not subset.

name: ARC-AGI-3
description: Interactive agentic reasoning benchmark — 1000+ levels / 150+ environments. Launched Mar 25 2026. $2M prize. Best frontier (Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview) 0.37%, humans 100%.

name: GAIA-2
description: Dynamic / async agent environments benchmark. Launched 2026 (ICLR/OpenReview). GPT-5 high 42% pass@1, Kimi K2 21% (open SOTA).

name: FrontierScience
description: OpenAI FrontierScience — 700 expert-level science (phys/chem/bio) Q (160 gold). Sub-tracks: Olympiad + Research. GPT-5.2 leads (Olymp 77 / Res 25).

name: KMMMU
description: Korean MMMU — 3,466 Q + 300 culture-specific + 627 hard subset across 9 disciplines × 9 visual types. Open-source 42.05% / proprietary 52.42% on hard subset (arxiv 2604.13058).

name: QIMMA (قِمّة)
description: Arabic LLM quality-first leaderboard by TII — 52K samples / 109 subsets / 14 source benchmarks / 7 domains, 6 prompt templates. Jais-2-70B-Chat tops ArabicMMLU.

name: SuperGPQA
description: Extended GPQA-style expert science questions, harder set. Hy3-preview 51.6.

name: Codeforces ELO
description: Estimated Codeforces competitive-programming ELO rating from agent runs on real contests. Gemma 4 31B 2150.

name: Claw-Eval
description: Xiaomi MiMo internal multi-tool agent eval — measures tool-call sequencing accuracy. MiMo V2.5 Pro 63.8.

name: USAMO
description: USA Mathematical Olympiad — high-school-level rigorous proof problems. Claude Mythos Preview 97.6.

name: PAI-Bench Text2World
description: NVIDIA Physical AI Bench — text-to-world video generation eval. VQA-based domain score across 7 physical-AI domains + 8 video-quality metrics. 0-1 scale. Cosmos Predict 2.5 2B/14B 0.768.

name: PAI-Bench Image2World
description: Image-conditioned variant of PAI-Bench. Cosmos Predict 2.5 2B 0.810 (beats Wan2.2-27B-A14B 0.806).

name: WorldScore Static
description: Stanford SVL unified eval for 3D/4D/video world models, static scenes. 19+ models evaluated. HunyuanWorld-Voyager 77.62 #1.

name: WorldScore Dynamic
description: WorldScore dynamic-scene variant evaluating temporal coherence. HunyuanWorld-Voyager 54.53.

name: WorldScore 3D Consistency
description: Cross-view 3D consistency sub-metric. WonderWorld 86.87.

name: EWMBench
description: Embodied World Model Benchmark — visual fidelity + physical consistency + instruction-action alignment for robotic world simulators. AgiBot Genie Envisioner GE-Base baseline (LTX 0.94 spatial, 0.98 temporal).

name: AV FVD (StyleGAN)
description: Frechet Video Distance — autonomous-driving sim quality. LOWER is better. Cosmos Predict 2.5 2B 23.06 (2.3× over Predict1).

name: AV FID
description: Frechet Inception Distance for AV-domain video frames. LOWER is better.

name: Robot Manipulation PSNR
description: Cosmos PAI-Bench robot-manipulation video reconstruction PSNR. Cosmos Predict 2.5 2B 24.95 dB.

name: Robot Manipulation FVD
description: Cosmos PAI-Bench robot-manip FVD. LOWER is better.

name: VLABench Track 1 — Primitive
description: ICCV 2025 benchmark — 100 categories, 2000+ objects, long-horizon language-conditioned VLA. Pi-0.5 47% on primitive track.

name: RoboArena ELO
description: Distributed real-world double-blind pairwise eval (4284+ episodes). Standardized cross-policy real-robot benchmark.

name: WorldModelBench Average
description: NeurIPS 2025 — 7 domains × 56 sub-tasks, 350 prompts. Instruction Following / Common Sense / Physical Adherence (67K human labels).

name: 1X World Model Sampling (PSNR)
description: 1X World Model Challenge — image-frame forecasting on humanoid data. 1X World Model 23.0 dB (1st place).

name: 1X World Model Compression (CE)
description: 1X World Model Challenge — top-500 cross-entropy on discrete latent prediction. 1X 6.6386 (1st place). LOWER is better.

name: Helix T_eff
description: Figure AI normalized package throughput vs human demonstrator. Helix S1 1.1.

name: Intuitive Physics Spatial Puzzle
description: Cosmos Reason1 sub-bench — physical scene spatial reasoning puzzles. Cosmos Reason 1 56B 85.2 post-RL.

name: Intuitive Physics Arrow of Time
description: Cosmos Reason1 sub-bench — temporal causality / arrow-of-time. 57.0 post-RL.

name: Intuitive Physics Object Permanence
description: Cosmos Reason1 sub-bench — object permanence reasoning. 54.8 post-RL.

name: ALOHA 4-Task Average
description: Bimanual ALOHA tasks (fold shorts/shirt, scoop/put X into bowl/pot). OpenVLA-OFT 87.8% leader.

name: SimplerEnv Average
description: Open-source robot simulator leaderboard. Octo-Base 62% baseline (50-75% range).

name: GR-1 Real Language Following
description: Fruit-placement language-conditioning task on Fourier GR-1 humanoid (NVIDIA GEAR).

name: Unitree G1 Post-train (1K demos)
description: Post-training success rate on Unitree G1 humanoid. GR00T N1.5 98.8% leader.

description: OpenAI 525-task clinician chat benchmark (care consult / writing & docs / medical research) — physician-authored rubrics

name: HealthBench
description: OpenAI 5,000-conversation health rubric benchmark

name: MedQA (USMLE)
description: USMLE-style multiple-choice question answering

name: MedMCQA
description: Indian medical entrance exam multi-choice

name: PubMedQA
description: Yes/no/maybe biomedical research QA

description: MMLU 6-subject medical bundle (anatomy, college medicine, clinical knowledge, medical genetics, professional medicine, virology)

name: MedXpertQA
description: Expert-level medical reasoning benchmark

name: MedBullets-5op
description: Clinical case multi-choice

name: NEJM Image Challenge
description: New England Journal of Medicine image-based diagnostic challenge

name: JAMA Clinical Challenge
description: JAMA Network clinical case quiz

name: Universal Med-Seg Dice
description: Universal medical-segmentation Dice score across 11+ modalities (CT/MRI/X-ray/endoscopy/ultrasound)

description: Ophthalmic foundation-model benchmark — external validation AUC

name: Pathology External Benchmark

name: MMedBench (Multilingual)
description: Multilingual medical multi-choice QA with rationale — 6 languages (English/Chinese/Japanese/French/Russian/Spanish). MMedLM eval

description: Large-scale Chinese medical benchmark (arXiv:2312.12806) — clinical diagnosis, prevention, BianQue/Sunsimiao/DoctorGLM eval

name: AMEGA Dataset
description: On-device medical LLM benchmark — clinical reasoning across multiple specialties. Med42 + Aloe lead on-device

name: BLUE (Biomedical Lang. Understanding)
description: 5-task / 10-dataset benchmark — sentence similarity, NER, relation extraction, document classification, NLI for biomedical/clinical text. BlueBERT reference

name: EchoNet LVEF AUC@50%
description: AUC at clinical LVEF threshold of 50% (heart failure indicator) on EchoNet-Dynamic

name: PanDerm (Skin Disease)
description: Dermatology FM benchmark across 11 institutions, 4 imaging modalities, 2M+ skin disease images

name: Polaris Clinical Safety
description: Hippocratic AI safety-focused healthcare clinical accuracy benchmark — patient-facing dialogue safety eval

name: Open Medical-LLM Leaderboard (avg)
description: HuggingFace Open Medical-LLM Leaderboard average — MedQA / MedMCQA / PubMedQA / MMLU Medical (anatomy, college medicine, clinical knowledge, medical genetics, professional medicine, virology)

name: CARE-QA
description: Clinical Assistance Reasoning & Evaluation QA — patient-physician dialogue tasks. Used in HealthBench-adjacent evaluations (Almanac RAG eval target)

description: Multimodal subset of MedXpertQA — 2,000 image+text expert reasoning questions across radiology, pathology, dermatology, ophthalmology, ECG

description: Biological lab protocol benchmark — find/correct mistakes in real laboratory protocols. bioRxiv 2024.08.21

description: % of predicted residues with high pLDDT confidence (>70). Standard structure prediction quality metric

description: Protein-ligand pose RMSD on PDBBind v2020 holdout — Boltz/Chai/AF3 docking benchmark (Å, lower=better)

description: MoleculeNet 17-task average AUC/MAE — standard chemistry benchmark (BBBP, ClinTox, HIV, MUV, BACE, ESOL, FreeSolv, Lipo, QM9, etc.)

name: TDC ADMET Group
description: Therapeutics Data Commons ADMET property prediction — 22 task average. ChemFM, MoLFormer eval

description: SNUH-released Korean medical law QA benchmark on HuggingFace (snuh/KorMedLawQA)

description: Medical VLM average across 14 imaging tasks — derm/endo/path/radiology MCQ + VQA. MedDr eval set

description: Comprehensive medical hallucination evaluation — expert-validated case scenarios + medical databases. UCSD 2024-12

name: MedRiskEval
description: Medical risk evaluation benchmark — user-perspective patient safety in healthcare LLM use

name: CSEDB Safety
description: Clinical Safety-Effectiveness Dual-Track Benchmark — 30 metrics: critical illness recognition, guideline adherence, medication safety. Average safety 54.7%, drops 13.3% in high-risk scenarios. Nature npj Digital Medicine 2025

description: CSEDB effectiveness track. Average effectiveness 62.3%

description: Patient-use safety benchmark — measures LLM safety when used directly by patients (vs clinicians). OpenReview 2025

description: Largest test dataset (10,000 studies) for radiology report generation eval. ReXrank's primary holdout

description: AfriMed-QA short answer questions — 1,200+ long-form clinical answers

description: Hospital 30-day readmission prediction on MIMIC-IV. Standard outcome prediction benchmark

description: AI4Bharat multilingual Indian-language medical evaluation — Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Bengali medical QA

description: BioMistral first large-scale multilingual medical LLM eval — 7 translated languages. CNRS Jean Zay HPC

name: UHN Clinical Benchmark
description: UHN AI Hub clinical evaluation — Toronto multi-hospital dataset. Vector Institute

description: Med42 internal clinical evaluation — comprehensive medical task suite (Cerebras-trained)

description: China Medical Licensing Examination corpus — 60K+ MCQs. Standard Chinese medical LLM benchmark

description: Chinese professional medicine QA — Tsinghua THUDM benchmark for GLM-Medical evaluation

name: MedHELM Clinical Decision Support
description: MedHELM Clinical Decision Support category — diagnostic + treatment reasoning. Models perform 0.56-0.72 on normalized accuracy

description: MedHELM administrative + workflow tasks — billing, scheduling, documentation. Models 0.53-0.63

description: MedHELM clinical note generation — discharge summaries, progress notes. Models perform strongly (0.73-0.85)

description: MedHELM medical research assistance — literature review, hypothesis generation. Models 0.65-0.75

description: Multimodal clinical agent — NEJM image-centric diagnostic cases. Multimodal accuracy ~29.2% baseline (low difficulty signal)

description: Virtual EHR agent benchmark — 300 patient-specific clinically-derived tasks, 100 patient profiles, 700K data elements, FHIR-compliant interactive environment

description: Large-scale MNIST-like biomedical image collection — 708,069 2D + 10,214 3D images across 18 datasets. CT/MRI/X-ray/OCT/Ultrasound/EM modalities
